title: uva-ursi vestita
intro: Uva-ursi vestita, also known as bearberry, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to northern Europe and North America, belonging to the heath family (Ericaceae). The plant's leaves and berries have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their high content of arbutin, a compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of uva-ursi vestita are used to treat urinary tract infections, as well as to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of cystitis and other urinary issues. Herbal preparations of uva-ursi vestita include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to support urinary health and provide relief from associated symptoms.
